symptoms.symptom_name: Facial and anterior chest burn with immediate blistering

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct history of the thermal injury.
Suggests thermal injury to the upper airway.

symptoms.symptom_name: Currently able to speak in full sentences

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring in isolation but does not exclude evolving airway oedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Singed nasal hair/eyebrows, carbonaceous oropharyngeal material, mild expiratory wheeze

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Facial burn with hoarseness and carbonaceous sputum together strongly indicate evolving inhalation injury requiring emergency airway assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency airway assessment and likely prophylactic intubation before swelling worsens
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Prevents sudden complete airway obstruction from progressive oedema.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Arterial blood gas with carboxyhaemoglobin level, IV fluid resuscitation per burns formula
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses for carbon monoxide toxicity and guides fluid resuscitation for the TBSA burn.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Discharge home with oral analgesia and burns dressing only
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously delay recognition and management of a likely evolving airway emergency.
